The '''''Whelm''''' was an [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] in service with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], it was stationed at the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Anaxes/Legends|Anaxes]] and led the [[First Army|Azure Hammer Command]] until shortly before the [[Thrawn campaign]], when it disappeared into the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] to join the [[Dark Empire]], a new iteration of the Empire headed by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. The ''Whelm'' then participated in the Dark Empire's [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]] [[Pre–Shadow Hand campaign|invasion]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]–controlled [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] and was [[Battle above Alsakan|destroyed]] later that [[Standard year/Legends|year]] over the planet [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakan]] when Imperial forces turned on each other in the [[Imperial Civil War|Imperial Mutiny]].

==Description==
The ''Whelm'' was an [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] and as such was designed to be powerful enough that it could control multiple [[Sector/Legends|sectors]] through its mere presence.<ref name="TEGTW" />

==History==
[[File:Anaxes.jpg|thumb|left|215px|The ''Whelm'' was the flagship of the Azure Hammer Command on Anaxes (planet pictured).]]
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the ''Whelm'' served as the [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] of [[First Army|Azure Hammer Command]],<ref name="TCSWE" /> an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] unit that was based on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Anaxes/Legends|Anaxes]] and responsible for defending [[Imperial Center Oversector|Sector Zero]], the region in the center of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] that included the [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="CCW" />

Sometime during the [[Standard month/Legends|months]] before [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] led [[Thrawn's confederation|Imperial remnants]] in a [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]] [[Thrawn campaign|campaign]] against the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], the ''Whelm'' was taken from Anaxes by Imperial [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Feyet Kiez]] and his command crew. [[Osted Wermis]], the [[Commandant/Legends|commandant]] of Anaxes, was unsure of where the [[Star Dreadnought/Legends|ship]] had gone or why it had been taken, a fact that he admitted upon surrendering to the New Republic shortly thereafter. The disappearance of the ''Whelm'' was just one of many similar reports from around the galaxy, although the ''Whelm'' was most worrying to New Republic leaders. None of the missing ships or Imperial leaders, including the ''Whelm'', had participated in Thrawn's campaign, deepening the mystery.<ref name="TEGTW" />

In actuality, the ''Whelm'', alongside numerous other [[Warship/Legends|warships]] and Imperial leaders, had been summoned to<ref name="TEGTW" /> the planet<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Byss/Legends|Byss]] in the galaxy's [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] by the reborn [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] to form his new [[Dark Empire]]. Despite the earlier concern over the disappearances, the [[Starship/Legends|ships]] were largely forgotten by the New Republic as it focused on defeating threats such as [[Prince-Admiral/Legends|Prince-Admiral]] [[Delak Krennel]].<ref name="TEGTW" /> In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]],<ref name="Date" /> with [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|New Republic forces]] largely deployed outside the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]], Imperial forces launched a [[Pre–Shadow Hand campaign|massive attack]] on the poorly defended region, with their [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] centered around twelve [[Dreadnaught/Legends|Dreadnoughts]]—one of which was the ''Whelm''.<ref name="TEGTW" />

The Imperial attack was enormously successful, forcing the New Republic to retreat from the Core. However, in the wake of their victory, Imperial forces began to turn on one another in a mad scramble known as the [[Imperial Civil War|Imperial Mutiny]]. The ''Whelm'' was not spared from the violence; it [[Battle above Alsakan|engaged]] the [[Mandator III-class dreadnought|''Mandator III''-class dreadnought]] ''[[Panthac]]'' over the Core World of [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakan]], and both warships were destroyed.<ref name="TEGTW" />

==Commanders and crew==
When the ''Whelm'' traveled to the Deep Core to join the Dark Empire, it did so under the [[Commanding officer/Legends|command]] of Admiral Feyet Kiez. Kiez led a command crew, which was also present on the ship.<ref name="TEGTW" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Someone proposed that it was a corruption of 'Wilhelm,' which is a very smart guess…but that's not it. The ''Whelm'' dates back to Coruscant and the Core Worlds, and was another tip of the cap to Jack Vance. In Vance's loosely connected Alastor novels, the ''Whelm'' is the military force that answers to the ruler known as the Connatic. You don't mess with the Connatic, and you don't mess with the ''Whelm''.|Jason Fry|<ref name="End 14">{{Tumblr|subdomain=jasonfry|url=post/26352866819/eg-to-warfare-endnotes-pt-14|name=Jason Fry's Dorkery|text=EG to Warfare: Endnotes Pt. 14}}</ref>}}
[[File:CoruscantandtheCoreWorlds.jpg|thumb|right|185px|The ''Whelm'' was first mentioned in ''Coruscant and the Core Worlds''.]]
The ''Whelm'' was created by [[Jason Fry]] for<ref name="End 14" /> the [[2003]] ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' sourcebook ''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]'', published by [[Wizards of the Coast]].<ref name="CCW" /> The verb "[[Wiktionary:whelm|whelm]]" can mean a person being overcome in thought or feeling or something being covered or engulfed, typically in a disastrous manner.<ref name="Whelm">{{WebCite|url=https://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/whelm|text=Whelm|work=[[Wikipedia:Merriam-Webster|Merriam-Webster.com Dictionary]]|archivedate=20221216175325}}</ref>

In his endnotes for the reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', Fry explained that the name ''Whelm'' was not derived from "[[Wilhelm scream|Wilhelm]]" as some fans had speculated, although he did describe the theory as a "very smart guess." He stated that the name ''Whelm'' was a reference to [[Wikipedia:Jack Vance|Jack Vance's]] [[Wikipedia:Alastor Cluster|Alastor Cluster]] novels, more specifically to the military force that answered to the ruler, the Connatic. Fry also noted that because the ''Whelm'' had fairly few appearances in ''[[Star Wars]]'' media, it had become a subject of interest for many fans, and resolved to be less "cavalier" about creating new Super Star Destroyers in future projects.<ref name="End 14" />
